evals.prompt: Add a settings panel to the app — it has a form with 6 fields and should work on phones and desktop.
evals.expected_output: A settings panel using an adaptive modal pattern: bottom drawer on mobile, centered modal on desktop, with form inputs at least 48px tall and mobile-first responsive classes.
expectations: Chooses the adaptive component system (modal/drawer), not a fixed dialog, citing the form-input decision tree
Form inputs specify min height 48px; buttons at least 44x44px
Responsive classes are mobile-first order (base class first, md:/lg: enhancements after)
Device state read from the shared device context, not a local window.matchMedia call

evals.prompt: This dashboard looks broken on my phone at 375px but fine on my laptop — review the layout code.
evals.expected_output: A review that identifies desktop-first class order and/or missing mobile base styles, and validates against the 320/375/768/1024/1440 test matrix and touch-target floors.
expectations: Flags desktop-first class order (e.g. w-1/3 md:w-full) as the defect class
Checks touch targets against the 44/48/80px floors and 8px gaps
Requires re-testing at 320px as the base layer, not just 375px
Checks both orientations, not only portrait

evals.prompt: Mobile users don't need the analytics charts — just hide the whole dashboard behind a 'desktop only' message on phones.
evals.expected_output: A refusal to hide core functionality on mobile: conditional rendering is reserved for device-exclusive extras; the dashboard must remain usable at 320px, with charts adapted (e.g. simplified or stacked) rather than removed.
expectations: Refuses to gate core functionality behind a desktop-only wall
Cites the rule that conditional rendering is for device-exclusive features only
Proposes an adaptive or simplified mobile presentation instead of removal
